title: Chewy Umami British Beef Fillet Bangers and Mash
description: Hearty one-pot dish featuring beef fillet and a rich, savory sauce, perfect for a cold winter night.
instructions: Season the beef fillets with salt, pepper, and thyme.
Heat a large clay pot or Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Sear the beef fillets until browned on all sides, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Set the beef aside and add the sliced onions to the pot, cooking until they're softened and lightly browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic, mushrooms, and tomato paste to the pot, cooking for an additional 2 minutes.
Stir in the beef broth, Worcestershire sauce, and Dijon mustard, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pot.
Return the beef fillets to the pot, cover with a lid, and transfer to the preheated oven.
Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the beef reaches your desired level of doneness.
While the beef is cooking, prepare the mash by boiling the diced potatoes in salted water until they're tender, about 15-20 minutes, then drain and mash with butter and milk.
To serve, place a beef fillet on each plate, spoon some of the rich sauce over the top, and serve with a generous helping of mash.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it rest for a few minutes before serving.
